title: Louis Monzies

infobox.value: (28 May 1849 – 13 March 1930) was a French painter and etcher. He was the curator of the three Museums of [Le Mans](/wiki/Le_Mans "Le Mans") for 10 years until his death.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

French painter (1849–1930)

| Louis Monziès | |
| --- | --- |
| Louis Monziès in his workshop | |
| Born | (1849-05-28)28 May 1849  [Montauban](/wiki/Montauban "Montauban"), [France](/wiki/France "France") |
| Died | 13 March 1930(1930-03-13) (aged 80)  [Le Mans](/wiki/Le_Mans "Le Mans"), [France](/wiki/France "France") |
| Known for | [Painting](/wiki/Painting "Painting"), [Etching](/wiki/Etching "Etching") |
| Movement | [Academic art](/wiki/Academic_art "Academic art") |

**Louis Monziès** (28 May 1849 – 13 March 1930) was a French painter and etcher. He was the curator of the three Museums of [Le Mans](/wiki/Le_Mans "Le Mans") for 10 years until his death.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/91/Lecture-Diderot-Louis-Monzies.jpg/250px-Lecture-Diderot-Louis-Monzies.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lecture-Diderot-Louis-Monzies.jpg)

*Lecture chez Diderot*, etching after Meissonier.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/03/Louis-Monzies-Villeneuve.jpg/250px-Louis-Monzies-Villeneuve.jpg)](/wiki/File:Louis-Monzies-Villeneuve.jpg)

*Villeneuve Lez Avignon*, watercolor.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Monzies&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Louis Monziès began to learn painting and etching in Paris in 1871 from the painters [Jean-Louis Ernest Meissonier](/wiki/Jean-Louis-Ernest_Meissonier "Jean-Louis-Ernest Meissonier") and [Isidore Pils](/wiki/Isidore_Pils "Isidore Pils"), and from the etcher [Léon Gaucherel](/wiki/L%C3%A9on_Gaucherel "Léon Gaucherel"). He got second-class and third-class medals in 1876, 1880 and 1881 at the annual [Salon](/wiki/Salon_(Paris) "Salon (Paris)") in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris").

He married Eugénie Alphonsine Courtignon in 1882 at [Cherbourg](/wiki/Cherbourg "Cherbourg") and three children were born : Jean in 1889, Pierre in 1891 and Jacques in 1895. He lived then in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris") but he owned also a small house in [Normandy](/wiki/Normandy "Normandy") near [Gréville-Hague](/wiki/Gr%C3%A9ville-Hague "Gréville-Hague").

Louis Monziès carried out etchings for illustrated books and for Art publications, among them, those after [Meissonier](/wiki/Jean-Louis-Ernest_Meissonier "Jean-Louis-Ernest Meissonier") and [Henri Pille](/wiki/Henri_Pille "Henri Pille"). He became a member of the [Société des artistes français](/wiki/Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_des_artistes_fran%C3%A7ais "Société des artistes français") in 1884 and also a member of the [Société des Peintres-Graveurs français](/w/index.php?title=Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_des_peintres-graveurs_fran%C3%A7ais&action=edit&redlink=1 "Société des peintres-graveurs français (page does not exist)") in 1891. He was quite popular in Parisian art [salons](/wiki/Salon_(gathering) "Salon (gathering)") with the [Hédouins](/w/index.php?title=Pierre_Edmond_Alexandre_H%C3%A9douin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pierre Edmond Alexandre Hédouin (page does not exist)") and in the literary gathering of the editor [Lemerre](/wiki/Alphonse_Lemerre "Alphonse Lemerre") who published famous living writers and poets with Louis' etchings.

Louis Monziès visited London and was named to the [Royal Society of Painters-Etchers](/wiki/Royal_Society_of_Painter-Printmakers "Royal Society of Painter-Printmakers") in 1894 when he received orders for illustrations for English books and publications. His etchings are included in major collections such as [The British Library](/wiki/British_Library "British Library") or the [Museum of Fine Arts Boston](/wiki/Museum_of_Fine_Arts,_Boston "Museum of Fine Arts, Boston").

But with photo-etching and other new publication processes, etchers were less and less able to make a good living from their skills. Louis Monziès move to [Le Mans](/wiki/Le_Mans "Le Mans") and became a painting teacher and a painting restoration specialist. He began to paint landscapes and urban environments in [Normandy](/wiki/Normandy "Normandy") and in other towns like [Venise](/wiki/Venise,_Doubs "Venise, Doubs") or [Avignon](/wiki/Avignon "Avignon"). He sold a lot of watercolors and oil paintings before the First World War. A few of these paintings were sent to the US by American officers housed by the painter at the end of the war.

He became the curator of the Museums of [Le Mans](/wiki/Le_Mans "Le Mans") in March 1920 and stayed so until his death in 1930.

## Illustrated books and Art publications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Monzies&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Illustrated books and Art publications")]

* [Barbey d'Aurevilly](/wiki/Barbey_d%27Aurevilly "Barbey d'Aurevilly") - [*Le bonheur dans le crime*](/wiki/Les_Diaboliques_(short_story_collection) "Les Diaboliques (short story collection)"), Société Normande du livre illustré, 1897 : etchings after [Regamey](/w/index.php?title=F%C3%A9lix_R%C3%A9gamey&action=edit&redlink=1 "Félix Régamey (page does not exist)")

* [Chénier](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9_Ch%C3%A9nier "André Chénier") - *Poésies*, Charpentier 1888 : etchings after [Bida](/wiki/Alexandre_Bida "Alexandre Bida"),
* [Claretie](/wiki/Jules_Claretie "Jules Claretie") - *Œuvres*, Lemerre, 1886 : frontispiece
* [Desbordes-Valmore](/wiki/Marceline_Desbordes-Valmore "Marceline Desbordes-Valmore")- *Œuvres poétiques,* Lemerre 1886 : frontispiece

* [Hugo](/wiki/Victor_Hugo "Victor Hugo") - [*Cromwell*](/wiki/Cromwell_(Hugo) "Cromwell (Hugo)")*,* Lemerre : frontispiece

* [Lesage](/wiki/Alain-Ren%C3%A9_Lesage "Alain-René Lesage") - *[Histoire de Gil Blas de Santillane](/wiki/Histoire_de_Gil_Blas_de_Santillane "Histoire de Gil Blas de Santillane")*, Lemerre : etchings after [Pille](/wiki/Henri_Pille "Henri Pille")

* [Mantz](/wiki/Paul_Mantz "Paul Mantz") - *François Boucher, Quantin* : etchings after [Boucher](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Boucher "François Boucher")
* [Martial](/w/index.php?title=Adolphe_Martial_Pot%C3%A9mont&action=edit&redlink=1 "Adolphe Martial Potémont (page does not exist)") - *L'Illustration nouvelle par une société de peintres-graveurs à l'eau-forte, sixième année*, Cadart 1879 : etching "Le joueur de Mandoline"
* [Michelet](/wiki/Jules_Michelet "Jules Michelet"), *Jeanne d'Arc*, Hachette, 1888 : etchings after [Bida](/wiki/Alexandre_Bida "Alexandre Bida")
* [Musset](/wiki/Alfred_de_Musset "Alfred de Musset"), *Œuvres*, Lemerre, 1878 : etchings after [Pille](/wiki/Henri_Pille "Henri Pille")
* [Perrault](/wiki/Charles_Perrault "Charles Perrault") - *[Contes de Fées](/w/index.php?title=Contes_de_ma_m%C3%A8re_l%27Oye&action=edit&redlink=1 "Contes de ma mère l'Oye (page does not exist)")*, Lemerre : etchings after [Pille](/wiki/Henri_Pille "Henri Pille")
* *[Portalis](/w/index.php?title=Roger_Portalis&action=edit&redlink=1 "Roger Portalis (page does not exist)")* - *Honoré Fragonard, sa vie et son oeuvre*, Rothschild, 1889 : etching
* [Racine](/wiki/Jean_Racine "Jean Racine"), *Œuvres*, Lemerre, 1874 : etchings after [Gravelot](/wiki/Hubert-Fran%C3%A7ois_Gravelot "Hubert-François Gravelot")
* [Sarasin](/wiki/Jean_Fran%C3%A7ois_Sarrazin "Jean François Sarrazin"), frontispiece
* [Scarron](/wiki/Scarron "Scarron") - *[Le Roman Comique](/w/index.php?title=Le_Roman_comique&action=edit&redlink=1 "Le Roman comique (page does not exist)")*, Lemerre : etchings after [Pille](/wiki/Henri_Pille "Henri Pille")
* [Shakespeare](/wiki/William_Shakespeare "William Shakespeare") - *Œuvres*, Lemerre : etchings after [Pille](/wiki/Henri_Pille "Henri Pille")
* [Uzanne](/wiki/Octave_Uzanne "Octave Uzanne") - *Caprices d'un bibliophile*, 1878 : etchings
* [Voltaire](/wiki/Voltaire "Voltaire") - *Romans*, Lemerre, 1878 : etchings after [Monnet](/wiki/Charles_Monnet "Charles Monnet") and [Marillier](/wiki/Cl%C3%A9ment-Pierre_Marillier "Clément-Pierre Marillier")

### Etchings for English books

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Monzies&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Etchings for English books")]

* [Brooks](/wiki/Geraldine_Brooks_(writer) "Geraldine Brooks (writer)") - *Dames and Daughters of the French Court* : portrait of Madame Valmore
* [Charles and Mary Lamb](/wiki/Charles_Lamb "Charles Lamb") - *[Tales from Shakespeare](/wiki/Tales_from_Shakespeare "Tales from Shakespeare")*, Heath & Co, 1908 : etchings
* [Chevillard Lenoir](/w/index.php?title=Chevillard_Lenoir&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chevillard Lenoir (page does not exist)") - *Celebrated Artists Sketches of Their Lives and Works*, Nims & Knight, 1888
* Comyns Carr - *Examples of Contemporary Arts. Etchings from Representative Works by living English and Foreign Artists*, 1878 : etching "Lady Lindsay of Balcarres" d'après [Watts](/wiki/George_Frederic_Watts "George Frederic Watts")
* [Hugo](/wiki/Victor_Hugo "Victor Hugo") - *Dramas*, G. Barrie, 1896 : etching after E. Bordes
* [Louvet de Couvray](/wiki/Jean-Baptiste_Louvet_de_Couvray "Jean-Baptiste Louvet de Couvray") - [*The amours of the chevalier de Faublas*](/w/index.php?title=Les_Amours_du_chevalier_de_Faublas&action=edit&redlink=1 "Les Amours du chevalier de Faublas (page does not exist)"), Société des Bibliophiles, 1898 : etchings after [Avril](/wiki/Paul_Avril "Paul Avril")
* [Musset](/wiki/Alfred_de_Musset "Alfred de Musset") - *All writings*, Edwin C. Hill
